title: Divide and Conquer Function (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the narrowing direction is reversed, base the return value marks on whether the final answer is consistent with the student's own narrowing logic.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is calc(); the Python function is calc(f). Both are correct.

The binary function f_N returns 1 if n >= N and 0 if n < N. The task is to find N using binary search in O(log n). The correct narrowing rule is: if f(m) == 1, search left (r = m-1); if f(m) == 0, search right (l = m+1).

rubric_points.name: Search bounds initialisation
rubric_points.target: l = 0 and r = 10^18 (or Long.MAX_VALUE), correctly bounding the search space for all valid N.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No bounds are set; the algorithm does not initialise a search space, or uses a constant unrelated to the input range.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Bounds are initialised but the upper bound is too small (e.g., 10^6 or 10^9), causing the algorithm to fail for N > that bound.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: l = 0 and r = 10^18 (or equivalent large constant covering all valid N), correctly enclosing the entire search space.

rubric_points.name: Binary search loop and midpoint
rubric_points.target: A while loop runs while l <= r, computing midpoint m = (l + r) // 2 at each iteration.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No loop is present; f is evaluated only once or a fixed number of times regardless of the search space.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A loop is present but the termination condition is wrong (e.g., l < r instead of l <= r, missing the case where l == r), or the midpoint is computed incorrectly (e.g., integer overflow from (l+r) without guarding, or uses l or r directly instead of the midpoint).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: while l <= r runs the loop until the search space is empty; m = (l + r) // 2 (or equivalent) correctly computes the midpoint at each step.

rubric_points.name: Narrowing: updating l and r based on f(m)
rubric_points.target: If f(m) == 1 (m >= N), search the left half: r = m - 1. If f(m) == 0 (m < N), search the right half: l = m + 1.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: l and r are not updated based on f(m); the loop does not narrow the search space and runs indefinitely or always returns the same value.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: l and r are updated, but the direction is reversed (f(m)==1 sets l=m+1 instead of r=m-1, and vice versa), consistently searching the wrong half.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: When f(m) == 1: r = m - 1 (N is at or before m, search left). When f(m) == 0: l = m + 1 (N is after m, search right). This correctly converges to N.

rubric_points.name: Post-loop return value
rubric_points.target: After the loop, the correct N is returned: if f(m) == 1 return m, else return m + 1 (or equivalently, return l).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: The return value is unrelated to the search result (e.g., always returns m or l without checking f), or returns the wrong boundary (e.g., always returns r).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: The return is close but off by one: for example, returns m unconditionally without the final f(m) check, which fails when the loop exits with f(m) == 0.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns m if f(m) == 1, otherwise m + 1; or equivalently returns l (which equals the first position where f is 1 after the loop exits).

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: calc correctly returns N for all valid inputs in O(log n), without forbidden keywords.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Returns incorrect N for most inputs, or contains forbidden keywords ('import', 'eval', 'globals').

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct N for simple cases (e.g., N near 0 or N = 1) but fails for large N due to insufficient upper bound or off-by-one in the return value.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct N for all valid inputs (0 <= N <= 10^18) in O(log n); forbidden keywords are absent.

buckets.label: Internally consistent but incorrect final result
buckets.description: Logic follows consistently from the student's own narrowing direction and return logic, but the result is systematically off (e.g., consistently returns N-1 or N+1).
